sections.summary: Title of the Codex standard: 'Standard for Mango Chutney (CXS 160-1987)'.
Records adoption and amendment history: standard adopted in 1987 and amended in 2019, 2022, and 2023.
Notes the 2022 amendment: changes were made to Section 8.2 on labelling of non-retail containers following CAC decisions in December 2022.
Explains the 2023 amendment: food additive provisions were revised and incorporated into the GSFA (CXS 192-1995) as part of aligning additive provisions with the GSFA.
Defines the scope: the standard applies to mango chutney offered for direct consumption (including catering and repacking) but does not apply when the product is intended for further processing.

Describes mango chutney as made from sound ripe and/or unripe fresh or preserved mango (subject to preparatory operations and optional ingredients per Section 3.1.2) and processed to ensure ambient-temperature stability; permits any suitable Mangifera indica variety and allows styles with pulp/crushed pulp and pieces, while other distinctive presentations are allowed if they meet the standard and are adequately labelled.

Defines the product more specifically: mango chutney is prepared from substantially sound ripe and/or unripe fresh or preserved mango (with essential characteristics retained), may include optional ingredients, and is processed and sealed as necessary to prevent spoilage and ensure stability at ambient temperature.
States varietal types: any suitable variety of mango (Mangifera indica L.) may be used.
Defines allowed mango chutney styles: (a) with only pulp or crushed pulp or both, and (b) with pulp and pieces.

Permits other product presentations provided they are sufficiently distinct from the standard styles, meet all other applicable standard requirements, and are clearly described on the label to avoid consumer confusion.

Sets essential composition and quality requirements for mango chutney: composition (mango/preserved mango and permitted optional ingredients) and minimum mango content, and establishes quality criteria including sensory attributes, pH limit, defect definitions and allowances; containers failing these requirements are 'defectives' and lot acceptance is by sampling with an AQL of 6.5.

Specifies composition: basic ingredient is mango and/or preserved mango, with optional ingredients including sugars or sweeteners, spices and herbs, food-grade salt, other fruits/vegetables and other suitable food ingredients.
States the basic ingredient requirement: mango and/or preserved mango.
Lists optional ingredients categories permitted in the product, including sweeteners, spices/herbs, salt, other fruits/vegetables and other suitable food ingredients.

sections.section: 3.1.2 Optional ingredients - a) sugars i  and/or foodstuffs with sweetening properties such as honey, ii  jaggery, date syrup
sections.summary: Permits sugars and other sweetening foodstuffs such as honey, jaggery and date syrup as optional ingredients.

sections.section: 3.1.2 Optional ingredients - b) spices and culinary herbs; iii
sections.summary: Permits spices and culinary herbs as optional ingredients.

sections.section: 3.1.2 Optional ingredients - c) food grade salt iv  or other edible salt
sections.summary: Allows use of food-grade salt or other edible salt as an optional ingredient.

sections.section: 3.1.2 Optional ingredients - d) other fruits and vegetables such as onion, garlic and ginger; and
sections.summary: Permits inclusion of other fruits and vegetables such as onion, garlic and ginger as optional ingredients.

sections.section: 3.1.2 Optional ingredients - e) other suitable food ingredients such as vinegar, tamarind, dry fruits and nuts, edible oil, etc.
sections.summary: Allows other suitable food ingredients such as vinegar, tamarind, dry fruits and nuts, edible oil, etc., as optional ingredients.
Requires the finished product to contain not less than 40% m/m mango fruit ingredient.

Sets quality criteria: mango chutney must have normal colour, flavour, odour and characteristic texture, pH not exceeding 4.6, and definitions for extraneous vegetable material and fibrous matter; the product must be reasonably free from listed defects and discolouration.
Requires mango chutney to have the normal colour, flavour, odour and texture characteristic of the product given the fruits and optional ingredients used.
Specifies a pH limit for the product: pH shall not exceed 4.6.
Defines defects: 'extraneous vegetable material' as vegetable parts that affect appearance but are not a health hazard, and 'fibrous matter' as visible fibre strands more than 1 cm in length.

Requires the product to be reasonably free from defects such as seeds or seed particles, peels, grit or other extraneous vegetable material, discolouration from oxidation, objectionable/metallic/off-flavours or foreign odours, and fibrous matter.
States that any container failing one or more applicable quality requirements (Section 3.3) or the minimum mango content (Section 3.2) should be classified as a 'defective'.
Provides lot acceptance criterion: a lot meets quality requirements when the number of 'defectives' does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5.

Permits use of acidity regulators and preservatives in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) for food category 04.1.2.6 (fruit-based spreads excluding certain subcategory) and restricts use of certain acidity regulators to those listed in Table 3.

Products covered by the standard must comply with the contaminant limits in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex-established maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Products should be prepared and handled in accordance with the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene and relevant Codes of Hygienic Practice and must comply with any microbiological criteria established under the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Food (CXG 21-1997).

sections.section: 6.1 It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene ( CXC 1 -1969 ) , 3  the Code of Hygienic Practice for Canned Fruit and Vegetable Products (CXC 2 -1969) , 4  the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low-Acid and Acidified Low-Acid Canned Foods (CXC 23-1979), 5 and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice
sections.summary: Recommendation that products be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and specific Codes of Hygienic Practice for canned and low-acid foods.

sections.section: 6.2 The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Food (CXG 21-1997). 6
sections.summary: Products should comply with any microbiological criteria established pursuant to the Codex Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Food (CXG 21-1997).

Containers must meet minimum fill requirements (product occupying not less than 90% of the container's water capacity minus necessary headspace, with flexible containers filled as commercially practicable); containers failing this are classed as 'defectives', and a lot is acceptable if the number of defectives does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of the applicable sampling plan at an AQL of 6.5.

Minimum fill requirements: product must occupy at least 90% (minus necessary headspace per good manufacturing practice) of the container's water capacity, flexible containers should be filled as full as commercially practicable, with non-conforming containers treated as defectives and lot acceptance based on an AQL of 6.5.

Minimum fill: a container's product should occupy not less than 90% (minus necessary headspace per good manufacturing practices) of the container's water capacity, and flexible containers should be filled as full as commercially practicable.
A container that does not meet the minimum fill requirement of Section 7.1.1 is classified as a 'defective'.

Lot acceptance: a lot meets the minimum fill requirement when the number of 'defectives' does not exceed the acceptance number (c) from the applicable sampling plan using an acceptable quality level (AQL) of 6.5.

Products must be labelled in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) with specific provisions for product naming (e.g., mango chutney or other composition-based names and 'flavoured with X' if an added ingredient alters flavor) and non-retail container labelling per CXS 346-2021.

sections.section: 8.1 The name of the product shall be:
sections.summary: Product name requirements: use the name 'mango chutney' or other composition-appropriate or customary national names, and where an added ingredient alters flavor the label must state 'flavoured with X' or 'X flavoured' as appropriate.
Labelling of non-retail containers should follow the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

Methods of analysis and sampling for checking compliance with this standard shall be those contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the standard's provisions.
Bibliographic notes listing the Codex standards, codes of practice, guidelines and recommended methods cited in the text (references 1–9).
